_meta.description: Steel AeroBlade Double Deflection Supply Grille, long front blades, 3/4in blade spacing, with opposed blade damper (per GRD schedule note 1)
_meta.nc_correction_applied: +7 NC added to every raw table value, per cutsheet note: 'For deflection settings of 22.5 or 45, increase stated sound levels by 1 or 7 NC respectively' (base table is referenced to 0 deg deflection)
_meta.model_correction_applied: 272 with damper = +0 NC, 1.00x pressure, 1.00x throw (no further adjustment needed) per Correction Factors for AeroBlade Supply Grilles table, page G22
_meta.target_nc: NC < 20 (raw table NC must be < 13 to survive the +7 correction)
_meta.target_nc_history: User initially asked for NC<15 (matching the ceiling family) but that target is not reliably computable here since the cutsheet's dash convention (NC<10) is coarser than the margin available after the +7 correction (would need raw<8). Settled on NC<20 instead, confirmed by user 2026-07-17.
_meta.size_ceiling_decision: Capped at the '24x24' nominal size tier (grouped with 48x12/36x16, core area 3.75 sq ft) per user 2026-07-17 - matches the family's pre-existing largest loaded Type
_meta.sizing_method: conservative - for a target CFM, find the largest tested velocity step where raw NC < 13; use that step's cfm as the max capacity for the group
_meta.min_cfm_logic: min_cfm = the cfm value 2 velocity-table steps down from wherever max_cfm's velocity sits (table steps: 300,400,500,600,700,800,1000,1200,1400 fpm), clamped at the 300fpm floor if max is already at 400fpm (no room for a true 2-step drop). Rationale: don't force a larger diffuser than needed just because it CAN handle more flow - avoid using a size 6x6 at a load that's barely above nothing when a smaller size could work fine down to some reasonable floor. User confirmed 2026-07-17: max stays at the NC<20 breakpoint, min is 2 steps down from THAT (not from some other NC reference).
_meta.min_cfm_clamping_note: 19 of 32 groups have max_cfm at 400fpm (only 1 step above the table floor of 300fpm) - for those, min_cfm is only 1 step down (the 300fpm value), not a true 2-step drop, since there's nowhere further to go on the table. Only the smaller sizes (6x6 through 30x6/18x10, which have max at 500 or 600fpm) get an actual 2-step drop.
_meta.group_note: Sizes listed together in one group (e.g. 24x6 / 18x8 / 12x12) share IDENTICAL performance per the cutsheet's own table structure - the bold size in each group is Titus's primary reference size; non-bold alternates are noted by Titus to 'vary slightly' but are treated as equivalent here per the cutsheet's own grouping
_meta.removed_types_reason: These existing family Types did not correspond to any Titus 272RL published nominal size - removed per user 2026-07-17 rather than fabricate performance data

size_groups.note: ceiling group - includes 24x24, the size cap decided by user 2026-07-17
